outlook.headline: UALink demand is building, but near-term momentum remains thin
outlook.summary: UALink is getting stronger design-in interest, led by Astera Labs and Marvell calling out hot demand and a rapid shift away from PCIe-limited scale-up. But the mention trend is still fading and one company only cited UALink as a standards reference, so this looks more like early adoption than broad near-term traction. Watch for whether current interest converts into tangible ramps into 2027, rather than just protocol chatter.

drivers.label: Hot adoption interest in UALink-class fabrics
drivers.detail: Astera Labs and Marvell both describe strong demand, indicating growing pull from customers evaluating scale-up networking alternatives.

drivers.label: PCIe limitations pushing a protocol shift
drivers.detail: Marvell says PCIe bottlenecks are driving a rapid move toward UALink/NVLink-class fabrics, a clear catalyst for purpose-built interconnects.

drivers.label: Early product ramp visibility
drivers.detail: Astera Labs expects UALink products to ramp in 2027 and cites multiple market opportunities, supporting a longer runway for adoption.

drivers.label: Broader protocol interest is improving
drivers.detail: Credo notes increased interest and demand around supported protocols, reinforcing that UALink is gaining attention beyond a single vendor.

drivers.label: Current mention momentum is soft
drivers.detail: Mentions are low and down vs. trend, and MaxLinear only references UALink as a standards topic, suggesting adoption is still narrow today.

risks: Design interest stays at the standards/specification stage and does not convert into bookings.
Broadening beyond a few bullish vendors remains limited if other companies stay neutral.
If PCIe bottlenecks ease or alternative fabrics win share, UALink momentum could remain contained.
